arts.ttl: Breaking: Stoke City confirm striker as Mark Robins' second January transfer signing
arts.sum: “We know there has been significant interest from other clubs and are delighted he has opted to join Stoke City. Ali Al-Hamadi is Stoke City's new number nine as he joins on loan until the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Ali Al-Hamadi to Stoke City transfer, 'bundle of energy and aggression' and why Ipswich are letting him go
arts.sum: Ipswich fans took to his personality and playing style very quickly after he arrived last January. Stoke City are turning to Ali Al-Hamadi as they try to find a way to get the team scoring more regularly in the all-important last three-and-a-bit months of the season.

arts.ttl: Gary Rowett delivers Stoke City verdict as he prepares for bet365 Stadium return
arts.sum: But he expects Mark Robins will see a similar climb in the right direction, telling the Oxford Mail: “Stoke are a big club. Rowett returns to the bet365 Stadium as manager of an Oxford United side who are unbeaten in the seven league games since he took charge, winning five.

arts.ttl: Stoke City close in on Premier League striker
arts.sum: Mark Robins has admitted Stoke need to bring in new players in the final week or so of the transfer window as they try to pull clear of the wrong end of the Championship while managing a long injury list. Robins has never suggested that the January window would be easy and the challenge for a club constrained by Financial Fair Play rules was put into sharp focus with the Cannon situation; relying on the loan market only to see a player then called back and sold for £10m.

arts.ttl: U’s boss gives strong response to rumoured interest in Tyler Goodrham
arts.sum: OXFORD United do not intend to cash in on Tyler Goodrham during the January transfer window, says head coach Gary Rowett.

arts.ttl: Exiled Stoke City striker ready to finally return to action after five months in cold
arts.sum: Stoke City exile Ryan Mmaee is finally back in full training as he bids to make his Rapid Vienna debut some five months after signing on loan. Mmaee made his senior breakthrough in Belgium with Standard Liege and played for Beveren and Aarhus, in Denmark, before moving to Cyprus then Hungary, where he was snapped up by Stoke in the summer of 2023.
